mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations, directly compressing forward S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns show no negative correlation with FEDFUNDS changes over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC exceeds 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 5-day returns remain positive in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE increases by over 0.2 percentage points.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based domestic demand growth, supporting corporate earnings and generating positive quarterly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce positive 60-day forward returns in over 60% of triggered cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects constrained output capacity, forcing firms to cut margins and driving negative monthly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening periods do not predict negative 20-day forward returns, with a walk-forward Sharpe ratio above 0.5 for long positions.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid inflation acceleration combined with stable federal funds rates creates a real rate compression that fuels speculative equity buying, yielding positive weekly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The combined signal produces negative 5-day forward returns in more than 50% of occurrences, or the interaction effect vanishes after controlling for VIX levels.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls sustains consumer spending capacity and corporate revenue growth, mechanically supporting positive quarterly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero or negative correlation with 60-day forward returns across multiple rolling 5-year windows, with Rank IC below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly real GDP contraction exceeding 0.5% signals an economic slowdown that reduces corporate profit expectations, causing negative monthly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are positive in over 65% of quarters where GDPC1 contracts by more than 0.5%, invalidating the slowdown signal.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production alongside falling unemployment indicates a supply-side recovery that boosts equity valuations, generating positive weekly S&P 500 returns during post-shock periods.
failure_condition: The joint supply-labor signal fails to predict positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or out-of-sample hit rate drops below 45%.
